  5 Jira ticket OMNI-1428 is an 'Idea' currently in the Backlog, assigned to and reported by Rajesh Dobariya.   8 Source: jira | Key: OMNI-1433 | Relevance: 7/10 | Mention: indirect | Updated: 2026-04-08T18:48:25.829+0800 | Status: For Pitching (To Do) | Type: Idea | Priority: High | Assignee: Vivian Lim Yu Qian | Reporter: Vivian Lim Yu Qian | Labels: compliance-mandate, unit-pricing, catalog-data, ccps-phase2, gmv-impact | polaris-merge-work-item-link: OMNI-508 | polaris-work-item-link: DPD-818
  9 Jira ticket OMNI-1433 is a high-priority compliance initiative mandating unit pricing for 47 product categories on FairPrice's e-commerce platform to meet Singapore CCPS Phase 2 regulations. Vivian Lim Yu Qian leads the assignment, but the work impacts Michael Bui's domain of product capabilities and catalog services. The project requires complex data engineering, including mapping SAP UOM to DBP categories, handling online-only carton SKUs, and ensuring marketplace seller data cleanliness. Success involves adhering to backend architecture standards and achieving an estimated $800K annual GMV uplift alongside regulatory compliance.

 11 ## [3/26] Achieve 100% GST compliance for Ecom orders 
 12 Source: jira | Key: OMNI-1361 | Relevance: 7/10 | Mention: indirect | Updated: 2026-04-08T19:30:22.450+0800 | Status: Discovery (To Do) | Type: Idea | Priority: High | Assignee: Alvin Choo | Reporter: Gopalakrishna Dhulipati | Labels: e-commerce-gst, architecture-decouple, slo-reliability, sap-integration, financial-compliance | polaris-work-item-link: PAY-6785
 13 Jira ticket OMNI-1361 outlines a high-priority architectural initiative to decouple DBP from SAP for E-commerce orders, aiming to achieve 100% GST compliance and resolve calculation discrepancies. The proposed design shifts all pricing, promo, and GST logic to DBP, making SAP a passive consumer, thereby eliminating single points of failure related to SAP downtime. While Michael Bui is not the assignee (Alvin Choo) or reporter, this initiative aligns with his governance mandate for backend architecture standards and production reliability in e-commerce. The project is currently in the Discovery phase; pending decisions on GL code mapping and integration methods (Comall vs. direct SAP) must be resolved before development can proceed.
 14 
 15 ## [4/26] Integrating Tencent's  Biometric Authentication (Palm Pay) solution with FPG App for member verification  
 16 Source: jira | Key: OMNI-1353 | Relevance: 7/10 | Mention: indirect | Updated: 2026-04-08T19:31:37.745+0800 | Status: Soft Prioritised (To Do) | Type: Idea | Priority: High | Assignee: Aditi Rathi | Reporter: Gopal Singh | Labels: biometric-auth, tencent-integration, checkout-friction, vendor-management, retail-platform | polaris-work-item-link: ENGM-2474
 17 Jira ticket OMNI-1353 is a high-priority Idea to integrate Tencent's Palm Pay biometric authentication with the FPG App. The initiative aims to reduce checkout friction by allowing members to bypass QR code scanning for membership verification at Cheers POS counters. A pilot is targeted for Punggol Digital District in September 2025, with a broader rollout planned for October 2025. While Michael Bui is not explicitly assigned (Aditi Rathi is the assignee), this vendor integration falls under his domain of e-commerce platform capabilities and architectural governance. A query from Danielle Lee on February 13, 2026, highlighted unclear ownership. Given the scope requires technical design review

Currently assigned to Prajney Sribhashyam with links to DPD-908 and DPD-944, the initiative relies on BigQuery customer tagging logic (covering ~60–70% of users). Recent updates from April 14 added specific dates to the program plan table for implementation phases. While Michael Bui is not directly mentioned, this falls under his governance scope regarding operational standards and SLOs for critical compliance risks.  95 ## [24/26] Tracking for all display content
 96 Source: jira | Key: LIGHT-34 | Relevance: 9/10 | Mention: direct | Updated: 2026-04-16T21:16:25.504+0800 | Status: Backlog (To Do) | Type: Story | Priority: High | Reporter: Nikhil Grover | Labels: ad-tech-tracking, osmos-integration, retail-media, architecture-governance, acceptance-criteria | cloners: LIGHT-70 | parent: LIGHT-394
 97 Michael Bui actively engaged with Jira ticket LIGHT-34 ('Tracking for all display content'), a High Priority Story under parent LIGHT-394 (RMN integration). As the engineering lead governing ad tech standards and backend architecture, Michael raised critical clarifications on OSMOS event verification logic and user consent requirements. He specifically questioned implementation details regarding timing resets when users switch browser tabs versus using mobile back-navigation buttons. His input evaluated the technical feasibility of tracking impressions based on viewport duration (1s for images, 2s for video) and click events across various display modules like banners and search bars. Michael's analysis suggests he is determining if the current acceptance criteria sufficiently handle edge cases before approving the move from Backlog to active development.

 99 ## [25/26] Web - OG Home - Testhas Offer swimlane - Ad label is missing for the first listed product
100 Source: jira | Key: RM-697 | Relevance: 9/10 | Mention: direct | Updated: 2026-04-17T14:14:23.334+0800 | Status: Done (Done) | Type: Bug | Priority: High | Assignee: Michael Bui | Reporter: Madhuri Nalamothu | Resolution: Done | Labels: ad-tech-bug, web-platform, e2e-testing, high-priority, retail-ads
101 Jira ticket RM-697 (Bug: High priority) was assigned to Michael Bui regarding a missing ad label for the first product in the 'Testhas Offer swimlane' on the Web OG Home page. Reported by Madhuri Nalamothu on Jan 5, the issue specifically affected web platforms while mobile versions functioned correctly. Michael actively engaged in the resolution process; on Jan 6, he requested verification noting a discrepancy where he observed the ad displaying, suggesting potential environmental or timing factors. The ticket was validated and marked as 'Done' by QE lead Milind Badame on Apr 17 following confirmation that the issue was resolved via E2E automation testing. This represents a direct ownership scenario where Michael was responsible for resolving a production-relevant bug impacting e-commerce advertising visibility and adhering to high-priority engineering standards.
102 
103 ## [26/26] Transition to Impression-Based Inventory & Multi-Banner Delivery
104 Source: jira | Key: DPD-838 | Relevance: 9/10 | Mention: direct | Updated: 2026-04-17T14:57:54.635+0800 | Status: TESTING IN PREPRODUCTION (In Progress) | Type: Story | Priority: High | Assignee: Chee Hoe Leong | Reporter: Nikhil Grover | Labels: retail-media, ad-inventory, slo-validation, architecture-pivot, production-governance | parent: DPD-385
105 Jira ticket DPD-838 tracks the high-priority transition of Retail Media Network (RMN) ad inventory from fixed tenancy to an impression-based model supporting up to 20 banners. Currently in 'Testing in Preproduction', the story involves orchestration across OSMOS, DBP, and MOE services. A critical architectural pivot occurred on April 15, shifting endemic boosting logic prior to slot deduplication within the 'au' service. This change directly impacts production reliability for key pages Michael governs: Omnipresence, Search, and Checkout.
106 
107 Although not explicitly tagged in this specific comment thread, Michael's mandate as Engineering Governance lead requires validation that modifications preserve SLO targets and prevent regressions. The feature flag (be_osmos_multi_banners_enabled) was split and deployed for testing on April 17 following UAT sign-off on April 16.
108 
109 Action Required: Formal confirmation from Michael is needed to validate that the modified logic does not pose risks to existing SLOs for key RMN pages before proceeding with production deployment.
